title: Galium ciliatum
intro: Galium ciliatum is a perennial herbaceous plant, commonly known as sweet woodruff, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. It has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and digestive problems, due to its sedative and carminative properties. The bioactive constituents of Galium ciliatum include coumarins, flavonoids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, sweet woodruff is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is used to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of restlessness.
taxonomy: Galium ciliatum is classified in the kingdom Plantae, with more specific classification as follows: it belongs to the family Rubiaceae, order Gentianales, class Magnoliopsida, and division Magnoliophyta. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its morphology and anatomy. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes many species of galium, as well as other related genera. Galium ciliatum is a member of the genus Galium, which is characterized by its slender stems and small, white or pink flowers. The classification of Galium ciliatum reflects its evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other plants in the Rubiaceae family.

The plant contains bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to possess medicinal properties. benefits: Galium ciliatum is said to have several health benefits, including its potential to reduce inflammation, lower blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis. properties: Galium ciliatum, also known as the fringed bedstraw, has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various therapeutic properties. Its leaves and stems are rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its medicinal value. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating various health issues, including skin infections and wounds.
